name: Marmara Megacity Earthquake
description: A magnitude 7.5+ earthquake on the North Anatolian Fault under the Istanbul metropolitan area destroys critical infrastructure, displaces millions, and stresses Turkey’s economy, insurers, and housing markets.
trigger_event: Major rupture on the Marmara segment of the North Anatolian Fault

istanbul_m75_rupture.event: M7.5+ earthquake strikes Istanbul urban corridor
istanbul_m75_rupture.detail: USGS and Turkish disaster agencies cite high probability of M7+ Marmara event; 1999 İzmit earthquake M7.6 killed >17,000. Istanbul hosts ~16M+ people in dense unreinforced stock.
istanbul_m75_rupture.source: USGS seismic hazard; AFAD Turkey historical catalogs

port_logistics_halt.event: Marmara ports and bridges impair national goods flow
port_logistics_halt.detail: Istanbul handles large share of Turkey’s trade; bridge and tunnel damage in scenarios reroutes freight, adding 20–40% inland transport time.
port_logistics_halt.source: World Bank disaster logistics studies; Turkish Ministry of Transport briefs

infrastructure_collapse.event: Water, power, and hospital capacity collapse in worst-hit districts
infrastructure_collapse.detail: WHO post-earthquake reports show mortality tied to medical surge; 2023 Kahramanmaraş sequence displaced >3M with widespread utility outages.
infrastructure_collapse.source: WHO Türkiye earthquake reports; UNDRR damage assessments

mass_displacement.event: 2–5 million residents seek temporary shelter outside hazard zone
mass_displacement.detail: AFAD camp counts after 2023 exceeded 300k sites; a Marmara event implies multi-million displacement per modeled scenarios.
mass_displacement.source: UN OCHA displacement estimates; Turkish Red Crescent planning figures

housing_stock_loss.event: Hundreds of thousands of housing units uninhabitable
housing_stock_loss.detail: 2023 earthquakes damaged >230k buildings in Turkey per government damage surveys; Istanbul’s building stock mix implies comparable or larger uninhabitable counts in M7.5+ scenario.
housing_stock_loss.source: Turkish Ministry reports post-2023; World Bank Türkiye rapid damage assessment

construction_material_shortage.event: Cement and steel prices spike as rebuild competes with regional demand
construction_material_shortage.detail: Post-2023 Türkiye saw cement price volatility; import bills for steel rose with reconstruction demand.
construction_material_shortage.source: TurkStat producer prices; TCMB sector reports

insurance_solvency_stress.event: Domestic insurers face claims exceeding reinsurance towers
insurance_solvency_stress.detail: Turkish Catastrophe Insurance Pool (TCIP) covers residential; large industrial and commercial lines hit global reinsurers. 2023 event produced tens of billions USD in economic losses.
insurance_solvency_stress.source: World Bank disaster loss estimates; Swiss Re sigma natural catastrophe reports

gdp_contraction_risk.event: National GDP dips and lira volatility spikes on reconstruction financing
gdp_contraction_risk.detail: TCMB and IMF work shows large shocks raise financing needs; 2023 earthquake contributed to growth revision and wider external deficit.
gdp_contraction_risk.source: IMF Türkiye Article IV; TCMB inflation reports

internal_migration_rent.event: Rent inflation surges in Ankara, Izmir, Antalya as evacuees arrive
internal_migration_rent.detail: Internal migration after 2023 tightened rental markets in receiving cities; Turkish CPI rent component remained elevated.
internal_migration_rent.unit: % YoY rent increase in hot markets (stress)
internal_migration_rent.source: TurkStat CPI rent; academic migration studies post-2023

personal_rent_burden.event: Your rent or mortgage + repair costs dominate monthly cash flow
personal_rent_burden.detail: Tenants in receiver cities face bidding wars; owners in damaged stock face demolition/rebuild uncertainty.
